title: Professional Services Agreement between Horizon Digital Solutions and Atlas Retail Corporation

drafting_sequence.clause_text: Horizon Digital Solutions ('Provider') shall design, develop, and deploy a custom e-commerce platform for Atlas Retail Corporation ('Client') as described in the Statement of Work attached as Exhibit A.

The project shall be completed within eight (8) months from the Effective Date. Provider shall deliver milestone reports every four (4) weeks. Final acceptance testing shall commence within ten (10) business days of the final delivery.

Client shall pay Provider a total project fee of $480,000 in six (6) equal monthly installments of $80,000 each, due on the first business day of each month following the Effective Date. All payments are non-refundable once the corresponding milestone has been delivered.

All custom code, designs, documentation, and deliverables created by Provider specifically for Client under this Agreement ('Custom Work Product') shall be owned exclusively by Client upon full payment. Provider hereby assigns all rights, title, and interest in Custom Work Product to Client.

Provider retains all rights in its pre-existing frameworks, libraries, tools, and methodologies ('Provider IP'). Provider grants Client a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free license to use Provider IP solely as embedded within the delivered Custom Work Product.

Provider warrants that all deliverables shall be free from material defects and shall conform to the specifications in Exhibit A for a period of twelve (12) months following Final Acceptance. During the warranty period, Provider shall correct any non-conformities at no additional charge to Client.

Provider's total aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees actually paid by Client. Provider shall not be liable for any lost profits, lost data, business interruption, or any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.
Both parties shall maintain the confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged under this Agreement for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure.

Either party may terminate this Agreement upon sixty (60) days written notice for any reason. In the event of termination, Client shall pay for all work completed through the effective date of termination at the pro-rata project rate.
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas. Any disputes shall be resolved in the state or federal courts of Houston, Texas.

Client shall have thirty (30) business days following each milestone delivery to conduct acceptance testing. If Client does not provide written rejection within this period, the milestone shall be deemed accepted. Rejection notices must include specific, reproducible deficiency descriptions.

Provider shall deposit all source code for the Custom Work Product into an escrow account with a mutually agreed third-party escrow agent within thirty (30) days of Final Acceptance. Client shall have the right to access the escrowed source code if Provider ceases operations or materially breaches this Agreement.

Any changes to the project scope, timeline, or budget must be documented in a written Change Order signed by both parties. Provider shall not perform any work outside the original scope without an approved Change Order. Unauthorized work shall not be compensated.

In the event that Provider fails to deliver the completed platform by the project deadline, Client shall be entitled to a full refund of all fees paid under this Agreement, including milestone payments for previously accepted deliverables.
drafting_sequence.contradiction_description: Clause 03 states all payments are non-refundable once the corresponding milestone has been delivered. Clause 14 grants Client a full refund of all fees including milestone payments if the deadline is missed. These create incompatible refund policies.

drafting_sequence.clause_text: Provider retains the right to use, modify, adapt, and redistribute all code, designs, and deliverables created under this Agreement as part of Provider's portfolio, for marketing purposes, and in future client projects without restriction or attribution.
drafting_sequence.contradiction_description: Clause 04 assigns exclusive ownership of all Custom Work Product to Client. Clause 15 grants Provider unrestricted rights to use, modify, and redistribute the same deliverables. Exclusive Client ownership is incompatible with Provider redistribution rights.

drafting_sequence.clause_text: Following the expiration of the warranty period, Provider shall offer paid maintenance and support services at mutually agreed rates. Client is under no obligation to purchase post-warranty support.
All disputes arising under this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively through binding arbitration administered by JAMS in San Francisco, California under its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules.
drafting_sequence.contradiction_description: Clause 10 assigns disputes to state or federal courts in Houston, Texas. Clause 17 requires binding arbitration in San Francisco, California. These specify incompatible dispute resolution mechanisms and venues.

drafting_sequence.clause_text: During the term of this Agreement and for eighteen (18) months following completion or termination, Provider shall not develop, design, or deploy e-commerce platforms for any direct competitor of Client in the North American retail market.

Provider shall not subcontract or delegate any of its obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of Client. Provider shall remain fully responsible for the performance of any approved subcontractor.

Provider shall maintain professional liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per claim and $5,000,000 in the aggregate, and shall provide certificates of insurance to Client upon request.
